title: Implement authentication and authorization middleware for all backend API services
description: Design and implement a unified authentication and authorization layer for all backend API services (Equipment Catalog, RMS, Finance, Vetting, Social Media Engine, and Morgan agent). Use JWT-based authentication with per-service secrets and role-based access control (RBAC). Define roles (admin, operator, customer, service-account, morgan-agent) and enforce authorization on every endpoint. Create a shared authentication library for Rust/Axum and Go/gRPC services. Ensure Morgan agent and web chat endpoints have appropriate authentication and rate limiting. Configure CORS policies for backend APIs to allow only trusted origins.
subtasks: Design a role-based access control (RBAC) model and enumerate all roles and permissions required for each service.
Implement JWT authentication middleware for Rust/Axum and Go/gRPC services, using per-service signing secrets.
Integrate the authentication middleware into all API endpoints, enforcing authentication and authorization checks.
Create a shared authentication library/package for reuse across services.
Update Morgan agent MCP tool invocations to use a dedicated JWT/API key with scoped permissions.
Implement approval gates for high-risk MCP tool actions (e.g., payment processing, customer vetting approval, social media publishing).
Add per-endpoint and per-tool rate limiting, especially for sensitive actions.
Implement authentication and rate limiting for the web chat WebSocket endpoint, including lightweight token or CAPTCHA verification, per-session message rate limiting, and input sanitization.
Configure CORS middleware for all backend APIs, allowlisting only the production frontend domain and local dev, and restricting allowed methods/headers.
Test all endpoints for unauthorized access, role escalation, and CORS misconfiguration.
Run OWASP ZAP or similar dynamic scans against all API endpoints to verify authentication and authorization controls.
acceptance_criteria: All backend API endpoints require valid JWTs and enforce RBAC for every request.
Morgan agent and web chat endpoints require authentication and enforce rate limits.
Approval gates are in place for high-risk MCP tool actions.
CORS headers are correctly configured and only allow trusted origins.
All endpoints reject unauthorized or improperly scoped requests.
Automated and manual penetration tests confirm no unauthenticated or over-permissive access.
All services pass dynamic security scans (e.g., OWASP ZAP) with no critical or high auth-related findings.

title: Harden Stripe webhook endpoint with signature verification and dedicated security controls
description: Ensure the Stripe webhook endpoint is excluded from JWT authentication middleware and relies solely on Stripe signature verification. Validate that the webhook secret is never logged or exposed in error responses. Apply a dedicated rate limiting policy to the webhook endpoint, and ensure it is not subject to CSRF protections that could interfere with Stripe's requests.
subtasks: Update authentication middleware to explicitly bypass JWT checks for the Stripe webhook endpoint.
Ensure Stripe webhook handler verifies the signature using the STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET and returns 401 for invalid signatures.
Audit all logging and error handling in the webhook handler to ensure the webhook secret is never logged or exposed.
Configure a dedicated rate limiting policy for the webhook endpoint, separate from general API rate limits.
Document the security model for the webhook endpoint, including why it is exempt from JWT and CSRF protections.
Test the endpoint with valid and invalid Stripe signatures and verify correct behavior.
Run a security scan to ensure no sensitive data is leaked in logs or error responses.
acceptance_criteria: Stripe webhook endpoint is accessible without JWT but requires valid Stripe signature.
Webhook secret is never logged or exposed in any error response.
Dedicated rate limiting is enforced on the webhook endpoint.
CSRF protections do not interfere with Stripe webhook delivery.
Security documentation for the endpoint is complete.
All tests for valid/invalid signatures and rate limits pass.
No sensitive data is leaked in logs or error responses.

title: Implement encryption at rest and in transit for all databases and sensitive data
description: Enable encryption at rest for PostgreSQL and Redis using storage-level encryption (encrypted PVCs) or built-in database features. Implement application-level column encryption for highly sensitive PII fields (e.g., credit scores, SSN-equivalent data, API keys). Enable TLS for all database connections and update all service connection strings to require encrypted transport. Create a data classification document identifying all PII fields and their required protection levels.
subtasks: Enable storage-level encryption for PostgreSQL and Redis PVCs, or use CNPG's built-in encryption features.
Configure CloudNative-PG to enable TLS for PostgreSQL server and update all connection strings to use sslmode=require or sslmode=verify-full.
Enable TLS for Redis/Valkey and update all service connection strings to use TLS endpoints.
Implement application-level column encryption for highly sensitive fields in the vetting, finance, and CRM schemas.
Create a data classification document mapping all PII fields across schemas and specifying required protection levels.
Update all backend services to use encrypted connections for all database and cache access.
Test database and Redis connections to ensure encryption in transit is enforced.
Run a security review to verify no plaintext PII is stored or transmitted.
acceptance_criteria: All PostgreSQL and Redis data is encrypted at rest (PVCs or built-in).
All database and cache connections use TLS.
Highly sensitive PII fields are encrypted at the application level.
Data classification document is complete and up to date.
All services connect to databases and caches using encrypted transport.
No plaintext PII is stored or transmitted.
Security review confirms compliance with data protection requirements.

title: Mitigate SQL injection risks in dynamic query construction
description: Ensure all user-supplied values in dynamic SQL queries (filtering, pagination, search, sorting) use parameterized queries. For dynamic ORDER BY and column-name filters, implement allowlist validation to prevent injection. Add integration tests with SQL injection payloads to verify defenses.
subtasks: Review all dynamic query construction in affected services and refactor to use parameterized queries for all user input.
Implement allowlist validation for dynamic ORDER BY and column-name filters, mapping user input to a predefined set of valid column names.
Add integration tests that attempt SQL injection payloads in search, filter, and sort parameters.
Perform code review to ensure no string interpolation is used for SQL queries.
Run static analysis tools (e.g., sqlfluff, semgrep) to detect unsafe query patterns.
acceptance_criteria: All dynamic queries use parameterized/bind variables for user input.
ORDER BY and column filters only accept allowlisted values.
Integration tests with SQL injection payloads fail to compromise the database.
Code review and static analysis show no unsafe query construction.
No SQL injection vulnerabilities are found in dynamic scans.

title: Enforce secure secret management and rotation from initial deployment
description: Replace all placeholder API keys and credentials with a secure secret management workflow from the start. Use External Secrets Operator with a vault backend (e.g., HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager) for all secrets. Enable Kubernetes etcd encryption for Secrets at rest. Add a pre-deployment check to ensure no placeholder values exist in production secrets. Document and automate the secret provisioning and rotation process.
subtasks: Remove all placeholder API keys and credentials from Kubernetes manifests and source control.
Deploy and configure External Secrets Operator with a secure vault backend for all secrets.
Enable Kubernetes etcd encryption for Secrets at rest.
Implement a pre-deployment check (CI/CD gate) that scans for placeholder values in all secrets.
Document the secret provisioning and rotation workflow, including emergency rotation procedures.
Test secret rotation for all types (API keys, database credentials) and verify zero downtime.
Run a security audit to confirm no placeholder or unencrypted secrets exist in any environment.
acceptance_criteria: No placeholder secrets are present in any environment.
All secrets are managed via External Secrets Operator and stored in a secure vault.
Kubernetes etcd encryption is enabled for Secrets.
Pre-deployment checks block deployments with placeholder or missing secrets.
Secret rotation can be performed with zero downtime.
Security audit confirms all secrets are securely managed and rotated.

title: Enforce secure file upload validation and malware scanning
description: Enhance the Social Media Engine's file upload endpoint to validate file magic bytes, strip EXIF metadata, generate S3 keys using UUIDs only, set Content-Disposition: attachment, and scan uploaded files for malware or embedded scripts. Validate image dimensions and integrity by parsing the image.
subtasks: Implement file magic byte validation to ensure uploaded files match declared MIME types.
Strip all EXIF metadata from uploaded images before storage using an appropriate library (e.g., sharp).
Generate S3 keys using UUIDs only, never incorporating user-supplied filenames.
Set Content-Disposition: attachment on all S3 objects to prevent inline execution.
Integrate ClamAV or equivalent malware scanning for uploaded files, or at minimum validate image dimensions and integrity.
Add integration tests for file upload with malicious payloads, invalid types, and path traversal attempts.
acceptance_criteria: All uploaded files are validated for correct type and magic bytes.
EXIF metadata is stripped from all images before storage.
S3 keys are UUID-based and do not include user input.
Content-Disposition: attachment is set on all S3 objects.
Malware scanning or image integrity validation is performed on all uploads.
Integration tests confirm that invalid or malicious files are rejected.

title: Harden Signal-CLI pod storage and access controls
description: Encrypt the Signal-CLI PersistentVolumeClaim (PVC) at the storage class level. Restrict pod-level access to the Signal-CLI pod using Kubernetes NetworkPolicy so only the Morgan agent can communicate with it. Add a backup and recovery strategy for the registration data. Run Signal-CLI with a read-only root filesystem and minimal Linux capabilities. Implement monitoring and alerting for unexpected access to the Signal-CLI service.
subtasks: Enable storage-level encryption for the Signal-CLI PVC.
Apply a NetworkPolicy to restrict access to the Signal-CLI pod to only the Morgan agent.
Configure Signal-CLI to run with a read-only root filesystem and drop all unnecessary Linux capabilities.
Implement a backup and restore procedure for the Signal-CLI registration data.
Set up monitoring and alerting for unexpected access attempts to the Signal-CLI service.
Test backup and restore of registration data and verify access controls.
acceptance_criteria: Signal-CLI PVC is encrypted at rest.
Only the Morgan agent can access the Signal-CLI pod.
Signal-CLI runs with a read-only root filesystem and minimal privileges.
Registration data can be backed up and restored securely.
Monitoring and alerting are in place for unauthorized access.
All controls are verified through testing.

title: Establish supply chain security: dependency auditing, pinning, and image scanning
description: Implement dependency lockfile enforcement, vulnerability scanning, and container image security across all services. Commit lockfiles (Cargo.lock, go.sum, bun.lockb) to version control. Pin Dockerfile base images to SHA256 digests. Add dependency vulnerability scanning (cargo audit, govulncheck, npm audit) and container image scanning (Trivy or Grype) to CI pipelines. Enable automated dependency update PRs with Dependabot or Renovate.
subtasks: Ensure all services commit lockfiles to version control and enforce their use in CI.
Update all Dockerfiles to pin base images to SHA256 digests instead of tags.
Integrate cargo audit, govulncheck, and npm audit into CI pipelines for Rust, Go, and Node.js services.
Add Trivy or Grype container image scanning to CI for all service images.
Configure Dependabot or Renovate to automatically open PRs for dependency updates.
Document the supply chain security process and review schedule.
acceptance_criteria: All services have lockfiles committed and enforced in CI.
All Dockerfiles use SHA256-pinned base images.
CI pipelines fail on critical/high dependency vulnerabilities.
All container images are scanned for CVEs before deployment.
Automated dependency update PRs are enabled and reviewed regularly.
Supply chain security documentation is complete.

title: Implement application-level audit logging for critical operations
description: Add audit logging middleware or service to record all finance mutations, vetting decisions, social media approvals/publications, and Morgan agent MCP tool invocations. Store audit records in a dedicated audit schema in PostgreSQL with append-only access controls. Ship audit logs to Loki alongside infrastructure audit logs.
subtasks: Design an audit log schema in PostgreSQL with append-only access controls.
Implement audit logging middleware in all backend services to record critical operations (finance, vetting, social media, MCP tool invocations).
Include actor identity, timestamp, and before/after state in all audit records.
Integrate audit log shipping to Loki for centralized observability.
Test audit logging for all critical operations and verify records are created and shipped.
Review audit log access controls and retention policies.
acceptance_criteria: All critical operations are logged with actor, timestamp, and before/after state.
Audit logs are stored in a dedicated, append-only schema.
Audit logs are shipped to Loki and visible in Grafana.
Access controls and retention policies are documented and enforced.
Audit logging is verified through end-to-end tests.

title: Enforce Kubernetes pod security standards and security contexts
description: Add pod security contexts to all Deployment manifests: runAsNonRoot, readOnlyRootFilesystem, drop ALL capabilities, no privilege escalation. Apply the 'restricted' Pod Security Standard at the namespace level using PodSecurity admission controller labels.
subtasks: Update all Deployment manifests to set runAsNonRoot: true, readOnlyRootFilesystem: true, allowPrivilegeEscalation: false, and drop all Linux capabilities.
Apply the 'restricted' Pod Security Standard label to all relevant namespaces.
Test all services to ensure they run correctly with the restricted security context.
Review and document any exceptions or required capabilities.
Run a security scan to verify compliance with pod security standards.
acceptance_criteria: All pods run as non-root with read-only root filesystems and no privilege escalation.
All capabilities are dropped unless explicitly required and documented.
'Restricted' Pod Security Standard is enforced at the namespace level.
All services function correctly under the new security context.
Security scan confirms compliance with pod security standards.
